author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto the end, a psalm of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Christ is the end of the law to every one that believeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How long, O Lord, wilt Thou forget me unto the end?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1) that is, put me off as to spiritually understanding Christ, who is the Wisdom of God, and the true end of all the aim of the soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How long dost Thou turn away Thy face from me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As God doth not forget, so neither doth He turn His face away: but Scripture speaks after our manner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now God is said to turn away His face, when He doth not give to the soul, which as yet hath not the pure eye of the mind, the knowledge of Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How long shall I place counsel in my soul?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is no need of counsel but in adversity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “How long shall I place counsel in my soul?” is as if it were said, How long shall I be in adversity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or at least it is an answer, so that the meaning is this, So long, O Lord, wilt Thou forget me to the end, and so long turn away Thy face from me, until I shall place counsel in mine own soul: so that except a man place counsel in his own soul to work mercy perfectly, God will not direct him to the end, nor give him that full knowledge of Himself, which is “face to face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sorrow in my heart through the day?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How long shall I have, is understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And “through the day” signifies continuance, so that day is taken for time: from which as each one longs to be free, he has sorrow in his heart, making entreaty to rise to things eternal, and not endure man’s day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?” either the devil, or carnal habit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Look on me, and hear me, O Lord my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Look on me,” refers to what was said, “How long” dost “Thou turn away Thy face from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear,” refers to what was said, “How long wilt Thou forget me to the end?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lighten mine eyes, that I sleep not in death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The eyes of the heart must be understood, that they be not closed by the pleasurable eclipse of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lest at any time mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil’s mockery is to be feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They that trouble me will exult, if I be moved;” the devil and his angels; who exulted not over that righteous man, Job, when they troubled him; because he was not moved, that is, did not draw back from the stedfastness of his faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I have hoped in Thy mercy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because this very thing, that a man be not moved, and that he abide fixed in the Lord, he should not attribute to self: lest when he glories that he hath not been moved, he be moved by this very pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My heart shall exult in Thy salvation;” in Christ, in the Wisdom of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will sing to the Lord who hath given me good things;” spiritual good things, not belonging to man’s day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will chant to the name of the Lord most high” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6); that is, I give thanks with joy, and in most due order employ my body, which is the song of the spiritual soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if any distinction is to be marked here, “I will sing” with the heart, “I will chant” with my works; “to the Lord,” that which He alone seeth, but “to the name of the Lord,” that which is known among men, which is serviceable not for Him, but for us.
